file.content:
## help: List all available targets
help:
	@ESSENTIAL="api-generate build-full dev help lint test-image-run test-full test-quick test-standard web-schema-sync"; \
	echo ""; \
	echo "Usage: make <target>"; \
	echo ""; \
	echo "Essential targets:"; \
	echo ""; \
	grep -E '^## [a-z]' $(MAKEFILE_LIST) | sed 's/## //' | sort -t: -k1,1 | \
		while IFS=: read -r target desc; do \
			for e in $$ESSENTIAL; do \
				if [ "$$target" = "$$e" ]; then \
					printf "  %-24s %s\n" "$$target" "$$desc"; \
					break; \
				fi; \
			done; \
		done; \
	echo ""; \
	echo "All targets:"; \
	echo ""; \
	grep -E '^## [a-z]' $(MAKEFILE_LIST) | sed 's/## //' | sort -t: -k1,1 | \
		while IFS=: read -r target desc; do \
			SKIP=false; \
			for e in $$ESSENTIAL; do \
				if [ "$$target" = "$$e" ]; then SKIP=true; break; fi; \
			done; \
			if [ "$$SKIP" = "false" ]; then \
				printf "  %-24s %s\n" "$$target" "$$desc"; \
			fi; \
		done

content.thinking:
The issue is that `$(MAKEFILE_LIST)` contains the Makefile name, and `grep -E '^## [a-z]' $(MAKEFILE_LIST)` prefixes each match with the filename. The `sed 's/## //'` removes `## ` but the filename prefix `Makefile:` remains.

The grep output looks like: `Makefile:## api-build: Build the API server binary`
After sed: `Makefile:api-build: Build the API server binary`
Then `read -r target desc` splits on first `:`, so target=`Makefile` and desc=`api-build: Build...`


That's why every line shows "Makefile" as the target and the essential targets check fails—nothing matches the actual target names. I need to strip the filename prefix from the grep output before parsing.

Using `grep -h` will suppress the filenames entirely, which is the cleanest solution here.

content.text:
The problem is `grep -E '^## [a-z]' $(MAKEFILE_LIST)` — when `$(MAKEFILE_LIST)` is passed, grep prefixes each match with `Makefile:`, so the line becomes `Makefile:## api-build: Build the API server binary`. After `sed 's/## //'` and splitting on `:`, the first field is `Makefile` instead of the target name.

Fix: use `grep -hE` to suppress filename prefixes.

input.old_string:
grep -E '^## [a-z]' $(MAKEFILE_LIST) | sed 's/## //' | sort -t: -k1,1 | \
		while IFS=: read -r target desc; do \
			for e in $$ESSENTIAL; do \
				if [ "$$target" = "$$e" ]; then \
					printf "  %-24s %s\n" "$$target" "$$desc"; \
					break; \
				fi; \
			done; \
		done; \
	echo ""; \
	echo "All targets:"; \
	echo ""; \
	grep -E '^## [a-z]' $(MAKEFILE_LIST) | sed 's/## //' | sort -t: -k1,1 | \
		while IFS=: read -r target desc; do \
			SKIP=false; \
			for e in $$ESSENTIAL; do \
				if [ "$$target" = "$$e" ]; then SKIP=true; break; fi; \
			done; \
			if [ "$$SKIP" = "false" ]; then \
				printf "  %-24s %s\n" "$$target" "$$desc"; \
			fi; \
		done
input.new_string:
grep -hE '^## [a-z]' $(MAKEFILE_LIST) | sed 's/## //' | sort -t: -k1,1 | \
		while IFS=: read -r target desc; do \
			for e in $$ESSENTIAL; do \
				if [ "$$target" = "$$e" ]; then \
					printf "  %-24s %s\n" "$$target" "$$desc"; \
					break; \
				fi; \
			done; \
		done; \
	echo ""; \
	echo "All targets:"; \
	echo ""; \
	grep -hE '^## [a-z]' $(MAKEFILE_LIST) | sed 's/## //' | sort -t: -k1,1 | \
		while IFS=: read -r target desc; do \
			SKIP=false; \
			for e in $$ESSENTIAL; do \
				if [ "$$target" = "$$e" ]; then SKIP=true; break; fi; \
			done; \
			if [ "$$SKIP" = "false" ]; then \
				printf "  %-24s %s\n" "$$target" "$$desc"; \
			fi; \
		done

toolUseResult.originalFile:
## Kapow Monorepo Makefile — delegates to component Makefiles
## Usage: make <target>

# Load .env if present — makes vars available to shell commands in recipes
-include .env
export VITE_SUPABASE_URL VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY

.PHONY: api-build api-run api-generate api-test api-test-unit \
        api-lint api-migrate-up api-migrate-down api-clean schema-gen \
        web-install web-dev web-build web-lint web-clean web-test web-test-coverage web-e2e web-screenshots web-storybook \
        web-schema-sync \
        lint audit hadolint \
        smoke-test smoke-test-with-server \
        api-image-build web-image-build test-image-build test-image-run \
        tools-build tools-test \
        dev dev-clean dev-prune dev-db-up dev-db-down \
        compose-up compose-down compose-logs \
        test-quick test-standard test-full test-fixture \
        fixture-sql fixture-json \
        build-full \
        spec-gen \
        demo \
        help

## api-build: Build the API server binary
api-build:
	$(MAKE) -C api build

## build-full: Build single binary with embedded web frontend (API + SPA)
build-full: web-build
	$(MAKE) -C api build-full

## api-run: Run the API server
api-run:
	$(MAKE) -C api run

## api-generate: Run GraphQL code generation
api-generate:
	$(MAKE) -C api generate

## api-test: Run all API tests
api-test:
	$(MAKE) -C api test

## api-test-unit: Run API unit tests
api-test-unit:
	$(MAKE) -C api test-unit

## api-lint: Run linter on the API
api-lint:
	$(MAKE) -C api lint

## api-migrate-up: Apply database migrations
api-migrate-up:
	$(MAKE) -C api migrate-up

## api-migrate-down: Roll back last database migration
api-migrate-down:
	$(MAKE) -C api migrate-down

## api-clean: Remove API build artifacts
api-clean:
	$(MAKE) -C api clean

## schema-gen: Generate common/schema/schema.sql from migration Up blocks
schema-gen:
	bash scripts/gen-schema.sh

## web-install: Install web frontend dependencies
web-install:
	$(MAKE) -C web install

## web-dev: Start Vite dev server (port 8080, proxies /graphql → :8081)
web-dev:
	$(MAKE) -C web dev

## web-build: Type-check and build web frontend for production
web-build:
	$(MAKE) -C web build

## web-lint: Run ESLint on web frontend
web-lint:
	$(MAKE) -C web lint

## web-clean: Remove web build artifacts and node_modules
web-clean:
	$(MAKE) -C web clean

## web-test: Run web unit tests
web-test:
	$(MAKE) -C web test

## web-test-coverage: Run web unit tests with coverage report
web-test-coverage:
	$(MAKE) -C web test-coverage

## web-e2e: Run Playwright E2E tests for web frontend (starts Vite dev server automatically)
web-e2e:
	$(MAKE) -C web e2e-test

## web-screenshots: Capture visual baseline screenshots across viewports
web-screenshots:
	$(MAKE) -C web screenshots

## web-storybook: Start Storybook dev server for UI components (port 6006)
web-storybook:
	$(MAKE) -C web storybook

## web-schema-sync: Regenerate web/schema.graphql and graphql-env.d.ts from API SDL files
web-schema-sync:
	cat api/graph/schema/schema.graphql \
	    api/graph/schema/auth.graphql \
	    api/graph/schema/data_sources.graphql \
	    api/graph/schema/echo.graphql \
	    api/graph/schema/field_names_gen.graphql \
	    api/graph/schema/institution.graphql \
	    api/graph/schema/preferences.graphql \
	    api/graph/schema/search.graphql \
	    api/graph/schema/search_fields.graphql \
	    api/graph/schema/similarity.graphql \
	    api/graph/schema/zip.graphql \
	    > web/schema.graphql
	cd web && pnpm exec gql-tada generate-output --tsconfig tsconfig.app.json

## lint: Run all linters (Go + web)
lint: api-lint web-lint

## audit: Run security audit on web dependencies (non-zero exit on high+ severity)
audit:
	cd web && pnpm audit --audit-level=high

## hadolint: Lint all Dockerfiles with hadolint (runs via Docker)
hadolint:
	@find . -name 'Dockerfile*' -not -path '*/node_modules/*' -not -path '*/dist/*' | sort | \
		while read -r f; do \
			echo "Linting $$f"; \
			docker run --rm -i hadolint/hadolint < "$$f" || exit 1; \
		done

## smoke-test: Run search smoke test against localhost:8081 (requires running API)
smoke-test:
	bash scripts/smoke-test.sh

## smoke-test-with-server: Build API, start it, run smoke test, tear down (requires DATABASE_URL)
smoke-test-with-server:
	bash scripts/smoke-test-with-server.sh

## api-image-build: Build the standalone API production Docker image
api-image-build:
	docker build -f api/Dockerfile \
		--build-arg VERSION=$$(git describe --tags --always --dirty 2>/dev/null || echo dev) \
		--build-arg BUILD_TIME=$$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ) \
		-t kapow-api \
		api/

## web-image-build: Build the standalone web frontend Docker image (nginx + Vite build)
web-image-build:
	docker build \
		$$([ -n "$$VITE_SUPABASE_URL" ] && echo "--build-arg VITE_SUPABASE_URL=$$VITE_SUPABASE_URL") \
		$$([ -n "$$VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY" ] && echo "--build-arg VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY=$$VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY") \
		-t kapow-web web/

## test-image-build: Build the all-in-one test Docker image (postgres+API+nginx)
test-image-build: hadolint
	docker build \
		$$([ -n "$$VITE_SUPABASE_URL" ] && echo "--build-arg VITE_SUPABASE_URL=$$VITE_SUPABASE_URL") \
		$$([ -n "$$VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY" ] && echo "--build-arg VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY=$$VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY") \
		-t kapow-test .

## test-image-run: Run the test image, exposing the web UI on http://localhost:8080
test-image-run: test-image-build
	docker run --rm -p 8080:80 kapow-test

## demo: Build test image, run it, and launch the interactive Playwright demo
demo:
	bash scripts/demo.sh $(DEMO_ARGS)

## dev: Start full-stack dev environment (PostgreSQL + API + web) with hot reload
dev:
	bash scripts/dev.sh

## dev-clean: Remove this checkout's dev database volume (forces fresh fixture load on next `make dev`)
dev-clean:
	@HASH=$$(echo "$$PWD" | md5sum | cut -c1-8); \
	VOL="kapow-dev-pgdata-$$HASH"; \
	CONT="kapow-dev-$$HASH"; \
	docker rm -f "$$CONT" 2>/dev/null || true; \
	if docker volume inspect "$$VOL" >/dev/null 2>&1; then \
		docker volume rm "$$VOL"; \
		echo "Removed volume $$VOL"; \
	else \
		echo "Volume $$VOL does not exist"; \
	fi

## dev-db-up: Start standalone dev database (PostgreSQL+PostGIS on port 5433, full dataset)
dev-db-up:
	docker compose -f compose/db.yml up -d --wait
	@echo ""
	@echo "Dev database starting on port 5433. Use:"
	@echo "  DATABASE_URL=postgres://postgres:postgres@localhost:5433/kapow?sslmode=disable"

## dev-db-down: Stop and remove the standalone dev database container
dev-db-down:
	docker compose -f compose/db.yml down

## compose-up: Start 3-service production topology (db + api + web) on http://localhost:8080
compose-up:
	docker compose -f compose/compose.yml up -d --build --wait

## compose-down: Stop and remove compose services and volumes
compose-down:
	docker compose -f compose/compose.yml down -v

## compose-logs: Tail logs from all compose services
compose-logs:
	docker compose -f compose/compose.yml logs -f

## dev-prune: Remove kapow-dev volumes older than 30 days
dev-prune:
	@CUTOFF=$$(date -d '30 days ago' +%s 2>/dev/null || date -v-30d +%s); \
	for vol in $$(docker volume ls --filter label=kapow-dev=true -q 2>/dev/null); do \
		CREATED=$$(docker volume inspect "$$vol" --format '{{.CreatedAt}}' 2>/dev/null); \
		VOL_TS=$$(date -d "$$CREATED" +%s 2>/dev/null || date -j -f "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S" "$$CREATED" +%s 2>/dev/null || echo 0); \
		if [ "$$VOL_TS" -lt "$$CUTOFF" ]; then \
			echo "Removing old volume: $$vol (created $$CREATED)"; \
			docker volume rm "$$vol" 2>/dev/null || true; \
		fi; \
	done; \
	echo "Prune complete."

## tools-build: Build all tools (samplesql)
tools-build:
	cd tools/samplesql && go build -o ../../bin/samplesql .

## tools-test: Run tests for all tools
tools-test:
	cd tools/samplesql && go test ./...

## test-quick: Fast checks — Go unit tests, TS type-check + build, ESLint (~10-20s, no DB)
test-quick:
	MAKE=$(MAKE) bash scripts/parallel-run.sh api-test-unit web-build web-lint

## test-standard: Full local validation — quick + web unit tests + Go lint (~30-60s, no DB)
test-standard:
	$(MAKE) test-quick && MAKE=$(MAKE) bash scripts/parallel-run.sh web-test api-lint

## test-fixture: Validate fixture data loads cleanly against current schema (spins up temp Docker PG)
test-fixture:
	bash scripts/test-fixture.sh

## test-full: Complete validation — standard + integration, e2e, smoke, fixture (requires DB + running services)
test-full:
	$(MAKE) test-standard && MAKE=$(MAKE) bash scripts/parallel-run.sh api-test test-fixture tools-test && $(MAKE) web-e2e && $(MAKE) smoke-test

## fixture-sql: Generate complete.sql.xz from JSON fixture data (requires Docker)
fixture-sql:
	bash scripts/gen-fixture-sql.sh

## fixture-json: Re-extract JSON fixture data from a live database (requires DATABASE_URL)
fixture-json:
	cd tools/samplesql && go run . --format json --output-dir ../../common/src/main/resources/data/tables

## spec-gen: Generate reference documentation in docs/specs/generated/
spec-gen:
	bash scripts/gen-specs.sh

## help: List all available targets
help:
	@ESSENTIAL="api-generate build-full dev help lint test-image-run test-full test-quick test-standard web-schema-sync"; \
	echo ""; \
	echo "Usage: make <target>"; \
	echo ""; \
	echo "Essential targets:"; \
	echo ""; \
	grep -E '^## [a-z]' $(MAKEFILE_LIST) | sed 's/## //' | sort -t: -k1,1 | \
		while IFS=: read -r target desc; do \
			for e in $$ESSENTIAL; do \
				if [ "$$target" = "$$e" ]; then \
					printf "  %-24s %s\n" "$$target" "$$desc"; \
					break; \
				fi; \
			done; \
		done; \
	echo ""; \
	echo "All targets:"; \
	echo ""; \
	grep -E '^## [a-z]' $(MAKEFILE_LIST) | sed 's/## //' | sort -t: -k1,1 | \
		while IFS=: read -r target desc; do \
			SKIP=false; \
			for e in $$ESSENTIAL; do \
				if [ "$$target" = "$$e" ]; then SKIP=true; break; fi; \
			done; \
			if [ "$$SKIP" = "false" ]; then \
				printf "  %-24s %s\n" "$$target" "$$desc"; \
			fi; \
		done

lines: while IFS=: read -r target desc; do \

content.text: Changed `grep -E` to `grep -hE` on both grep lines. The `-h` flag suppresses the filename prefix, so the parsed target names will be correct and the essential targets filter will work.

file.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Launch the full-stack test container and run the visible Playwright demo.
# Usage:
#   ./scripts/demo.sh              # auto-advance (3s pause)
#   ./scripts/demo.sh --manual     # click to advance
#   ./scripts/demo.sh --headless   # headless smoke test
#
# All arguments are forwarded to the demo runner (pnpm demo).
# Logs are written to a temp directory printed at startup.

set -euo pipefail

CONTAINER_NAME="kapow-demo"
HOST_PORT=8080
MAX_WAIT=60  # seconds to wait for the container to become healthy

LOGDIR=$(mktemp -d "${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/kapow-demo.XXXXXXXXXX")
BUILD_LOG="$LOGDIR/build.log"
CONTAINER_LOG="$LOGDIR/container.log"
DEMO_LOG="$LOGDIR/demo.log"

echo "Demo logs: $LOGDIR"

cleanup() {
  echo ""
  # Capture container logs before stopping
  docker logs "$CONTAINER_NAME" >"$CONTAINER_LOG" 2>&1 || true
  echo "Stopping demo container..."
  docker rm -f "$CONTAINER_NAME" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
  echo "Logs saved to: $LOGDIR"
}
trap cleanup EXIT

cd "$(git -C "$(dirname "$0")" rev-parse --show-toplevel)"

# --- Build the test image ----------------------------------------------------

echo -n "Building test image..."
if ! make test-image-build >"$BUILD_LOG" 2>&1; then
  echo " FAILED"
  echo "Build failed. Last 20 lines:"
  tail -20 "$BUILD_LOG"
  echo "Full log: $BUILD_LOG"
  exit 1
fi
echo " done"

# --- Start the container -----------------------------------------------------

# Remove any leftover container from a previous run
docker rm -f "$CONTAINER_NAME" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true

echo -n "Starting container on http://localhost:${HOST_PORT}..."
docker run -d --rm --name "$CONTAINER_NAME" -p "${HOST_PORT}:80" kapow-test >/dev/null
echo " started"

# --- Wait for the app to be ready --------------------------------------------

echo -n "Waiting for the app to be ready"
elapsed=0
while [ "$elapsed" -lt "$MAX_WAIT" ]; do
  if curl -sf "http://localhost:${HOST_PORT}/" -o /dev/null 2>/dev/null; then
    echo " ready! (${elapsed}s)"
    break
  fi
  echo -n "."
  sleep 1
  elapsed=$((elapsed + 1))
done

if [ "$elapsed" -ge "$MAX_WAIT" ]; then
  echo " timed out after ${MAX_WAIT}s"
  docker logs "$CONTAINER_NAME" >"$CONTAINER_LOG" 2>&1 || true
  echo "Container logs: $CONTAINER_LOG"
  exit 1
fi

# --- Stream container logs to file in background ----------------------------

docker logs -f "$CONTAINER_NAME" >"$CONTAINER_LOG" 2>&1 &

# --- Run the demo ------------------------------------------------------------

cd web
pnpm demo "$@" 2>"$DEMO_LOG"

file.content:
#!/bin/sh
# entrypoint.sh — first-boot data loading, then supervisord
set -e

PGDATA=/var/lib/postgresql/data
DB_NAME=kapow
DB_USER=postgres
FIXTURE=/data/complete.sql.xz

# ── 1. Initialize PostgreSQL data directory if not already done ───────────────
if [ ! -f "$PGDATA/PG_VERSION" ]; then
    echo "==> Initializing PostgreSQL data directory..."
    su-exec postgres initdb \
        --encoding=UTF8 \
        --locale=C \
        --auth=trust \
        -D "$PGDATA"
    # Ensure postgres listens on TCP for the API and psql commands below
    echo "listen_addresses = 'localhost'" >> "$PGDATA/postgresql.conf"
fi

# ── 2. Start postgres temporarily for first-boot setup ───────────────────────
echo "==> Starting PostgreSQL (init phase)..."
su-exec postgres pg_ctl -D "$PGDATA" -w -t 60 start

# ── 3. Create database if it doesn't exist ───────────────────────────────────
psql -U "$DB_USER" -tc "SELECT 1 FROM pg_database WHERE datname='$DB_NAME'" \
    | grep -q 1 \
    || psql -U "$DB_USER" -c "CREATE DATABASE $DB_NAME"

# ── 4. Load fixture on first boot (sentinel: search_base schema) ─────────────
LOADED=$(psql -U "$DB_USER" -d "$DB_NAME" -tAc \
    "SELECT COUNT(*) FROM pg_namespace WHERE nspname='search_base'" 2>/dev/null || echo 0)

if [ "$LOADED" = "0" ]; then
    echo "==> Loading fixture data — this may take a few minutes..."
    # Filter psql meta-commands (\foo) that aren't the COPY terminator (\.)
    # to match what testdb.loadFixtureSQL does in Go.
    xz -dc "$FIXTURE" \
        | awk '{ if (substr($0,1,1) == "\\" && $0 != "\\.") next; print }' \
        | psql -U "$DB_USER" -d "$DB_NAME" -v ON_ERROR_STOP=0 --no-psqlrc -f -
    echo "==> Fixture loaded."
else
    echo "==> Data already present, skipping fixture import."
fi

# ── 5. Stop the init postgres instance cleanly ───────────────────────────────
echo "==> Stopping init PostgreSQL..."
su-exec postgres pg_ctl -D "$PGDATA" -m fast -w stop

# ── 6. Hand off to supervisord (manages postgres + API + nginx) ───────────────
echo "==> Starting supervisord..."
exec supervisord -n -c /etc/supervisord.conf

content.thinking:
The container log shows the fixture loading (COPY commands, CREATE INDEX, etc.) but it ends at the CREATE INDEX lines - it never shows "==> Fixture loaded.", "==> Stopping init PostgreSQL...", or "==> Starting supervisord...". 

The container log ends abruptly after the CREATE INDEX statements. The fixture load seems to hang or take too long. The entrypoint script filters psql meta-commands with awk, then pipes to psql. After the indexes are created, perhaps the fixture SQL has more content that's causing issues.

Wait, let me look more carefully. The container log shows many CREATE INDEX lines at the end. The `echo "==> Fixture loaded."` should appear after psql finishes. The fixture load might be taking too long (>60s) or the psql command might be hanging.
 
So the real problem is that the fixture loading step is exceeding the 60-second timeout before supervisord even starts. The entrypoint runs postgres initialization, loads the fixtures, stops postgres, then starts supervisord with postgres, the API, and nginx. The health check curl request only succeeds once nginx is running, but if the fixture load takes longer than 60 seconds, the container gets killed before reaching that point. I need to either increase the timeout or optimize the fixture loading. The `--rm` flag auto-removes the container when it stops, but since it timed out while still running, the cleanup should be able to grab the logs before removing it. The real issue is that the fixture loading—especially the index creation—is just taking longer than the 60-second timeout allows, so I should either bump MAX_WAIT to 120 or 180 seconds, or pre-load the database during the Docker image build to avoid the delay at startup.

But the user also mentioned the browser never launched, which is a separate problem from the timeout. The `pnpm demo` script should trigger Playwright, but it never gets there because the container times out first. Let me focus on fixing the timeout as the primary blocker, then see if there are other issues once that's resolved.

Looking at the container logs, they stop at CREATE INDEX without showing the "Fixture loaded" message, which means the fixture loading was still running when the 60-second timeout hit. The fixture data itself is what's slowing things down. I could pre-load the fixture in the Docker image to speed up startup, but that's a bigger refactor. For now, I'll bump MAX_WAIT to 180 seconds and see if that gives the fixture loading enough time to complete.

toolUseResult.originalFile:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Launch the full-stack test container and run the visible Playwright demo.
# Usage:
#   ./scripts/demo.sh              # auto-advance (3s pause)
#   ./scripts/demo.sh --manual     # click to advance
#   ./scripts/demo.sh --headless   # headless smoke test
#
# All arguments are forwarded to the demo runner (pnpm demo).
# Logs are written to a temp directory printed at startup.

set -euo pipefail

CONTAINER_NAME="kapow-demo"
HOST_PORT=8080
MAX_WAIT=60  # seconds to wait for the container to become healthy

LOGDIR=$(mktemp -d "${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/kapow-demo.XXXXXXXXXX")
BUILD_LOG="$LOGDIR/build.log"
CONTAINER_LOG="$LOGDIR/container.log"
DEMO_LOG="$LOGDIR/demo.log"

echo "Demo logs: $LOGDIR"

cleanup() {
  echo ""
  # Capture container logs before stopping
  docker logs "$CONTAINER_NAME" >"$CONTAINER_LOG" 2>&1 || true
  echo "Stopping demo container..."
  docker rm -f "$CONTAINER_NAME" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
  echo "Logs saved to: $LOGDIR"
}
trap cleanup EXIT

cd "$(git -C "$(dirname "$0")" rev-parse --show-toplevel)"

# --- Build the test image ----------------------------------------------------

echo -n "Building test image..."
if ! make test-image-build >"$BUILD_LOG" 2>&1; then
  echo " FAILED"
  echo "Build failed. Last 20 lines:"
  tail -20 "$BUILD_LOG"
  echo "Full log: $BUILD_LOG"
  exit 1
fi
echo " done"

# --- Start the container -----------------------------------------------------

# Remove any leftover container from a previous run
docker rm -f "$CONTAINER_NAME" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true

echo -n "Starting container on http://localhost:${HOST_PORT}..."
docker run -d --rm --name "$CONTAINER_NAME" -p "${HOST_PORT}:80" kapow-test >/dev/null
echo " started"

# --- Wait for the app to be ready --------------------------------------------

echo -n "Waiting for the app to be ready"
elapsed=0
while [ "$elapsed" -lt "$MAX_WAIT" ]; do
  if curl -sf "http://localhost:${HOST_PORT}/" -o /dev/null 2>/dev/null; then
    echo " ready! (${elapsed}s)"
    break
  fi
  echo -n "."
  sleep 1
  elapsed=$((elapsed + 1))
done

if [ "$elapsed" -ge "$MAX_WAIT" ]; then
  echo " timed out after ${MAX_WAIT}s"
  docker logs "$CONTAINER_NAME" >"$CONTAINER_LOG" 2>&1 || true
  echo "Container logs: $CONTAINER_LOG"
  exit 1
fi

# --- Stream container logs to file in background ----------------------------

docker logs -f "$CONTAINER_NAME" >"$CONTAINER_LOG" 2>&1 &

# --- Run the demo ------------------------------------------------------------

cd web
pnpm demo "$@" 2>"$DEMO_LOG"

lines: -MAX_WAIT=60  # seconds to wait for the container to become healthy
+MAX_WAIT=120  # seconds to wait for the container to become healthy

content.text:
The demo code looks correct — it calls `launcher.launch({ headless: false })` which should open a browser window. The issue is simply that the script never got to run `pnpm demo` because the health check timed out first.

The fix is just the timeout increase (60s → 120s). The fixture load takes ~73s, so 60s was too short. With 120s there's plenty of margin.

Want me to commit this?

    46→      await page.locator('button[type="submit"]:has-text("Search")').click()
    47→      await page.waitForURL('**/search?**', { timeout: 10000 })
    48→      // Wait for results table to load
    49→      await page.waitForSelector('table', { timeout: 10000 }).catch(() => {
    50→        // Results may be empty — that's OK for the demo
    51→      })
    52→    },
    53→  },
    54→

The "Submit Search" step (4) catches the case where no table appears, but then "Browse Results" (step 5) unconditionally waits for a `<table>`. If the search returned no results (or the table didn't render), step 5 times out.

Let me check how results are actually rendered — it might not use a `<table>` element.

1→import { useEffect, useMemo, useState, useCallback } from 'react'
     2→import { useSearchParams } from 'react-router-dom'
     3→import { graphql } from 'gql.tada'
     4→import { useQuery } from '@/lib/graphql'
     5→import {
     6→  Box,
     7→  Button,
     8→  Collapse,
     9→  Container,
    10→  Flex,
    11→  Group,
    12→  NativeSelect,
    13→  Pagination,
    14→  SimpleGrid,
    15→  Stack,
    16→  Text,
    17→  Title,
    18→} from '@mantine/core'
    19→import { useDisclosure, useMediaQuery } from '@mantine/hooks'
    20→import { Download, FileDown, SlidersHorizontal } from 'lucide-react'
    21→import {
    22→  type SearchField,
    23→  type SearchInstitution,
    24→  type SearchOutputField,
    25→  type UserLocationValue,
    26→  type ViewMode,
    27→  fieldsByTheme,
    28→  buildFilterInputs,
    29→  serializeFilterValue,
    30→  parseUrlToFilters,
    31→  parseBlendParam,
    32→  serializeBlendWeights,
    33→  FilterControl,
    34→  LocationInput,
    35→  InstitutionDetail,
    36→  InstitutionCard,
    37→  InstitutionCardSkeleton,
    38→  ResultsList,
    39→  ResultsMap,
    40→  ResultsTable,
    41→  ColumnPicker,
    42→  ViewToggle,
    43→  downloadSearchPdf,
    44→  RankingBlendBuilder,
    45→  type SortState,
    46→} from '@/components/search'
    47→import { useDataSources } from '@/components/search/useDataSources'
    48→
    49→// ── Constants ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    50→
    51→const MAX_RESULTS = 100
    52→const PAGE_SIZE_OPTIONS = [5, 10, 20, 50, 100]
    53→const DEFAULT_PAGE_SIZE = 20
    54→const VISIBLE_COLUMNS_KEY = 'kapow:visible-columns'
    55→const COLUMN_ORDER_KEY = 'kapow:column-order'
    56→const VIEW_MODE_KEY = 'kapow:results-view'
    57→const VALID_VIEW_MODES: ViewMode[] = ['card', 'list', 'table', 'map']
    58→
    59→// ── GraphQL queries ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    60→
    61→const SearchFieldsQuery = graphql(`
    62→  query SearchFields {
    63→    searchFields {
    64→      __typename
    65→      name
    66→      label
    67→      description
    68→      theme
    69→      visibility
    70→      searchability
    71→      exportable
    72→      dataSourceID
    73→      ... on ChoiceSearchField {
    74→        options {
    75→          value
    76→          displayName
    77→        }
    78→        minSelections
    79→        maxSelections
    80→      }
    81→      ... on NumericalSearchField {
    82→        min
    83→        max
    84→        step
    85→        unit
    86→        defaultValue
    87→      }
    88→      ... on NumericalRangeSearchField {
    89→        min
    90→        max
    91→        step
    92→        unit
    93→        defaultLower
    94→        defaultUpper
    95→      }
    96→      ... on GeoSearchField {
    97→        defaultLatitude
    98→        defaultLongitude
    99→        maxRadiusMiles
   100→      }
   101→    }
   102→  }
   103→`)
   104→
   105→const SearchOutputFieldsQuery = graphql(`
   106→  query SearchOutputFields {
   107→    searchOutputFields {
   108→      name
   109→      label
   110→      theme
   111→      defaultVisible
   112→      exportable
   113→      dataSourceID
   114→      sortable
   115→      unit
   116→      outputKey
   117→    }
   118→  }
   119→`)
   120→
   121→const SearchQuery = graphql(`
   122→  query Search($filters: [FilterInput!], $limit: Int, $offset: Int, $sort: SortInput, $userLocation: UserLocation, $rankingBlend: [RankingBlendWeight!]) {
   123→    search(filters: $filters, limit: $limit, offset: $offset, sort: $sort, userLocation: $userLocation, rankingBlend: $rankingBlend) {
   124→      results {
   125→        unitID
   126→        name
   127→        city
   128→        state
   129→        latitude
   130→        longitude
   131→        distance
   132→        myPrice
   133→        blendScore
   134→        locationDetails {
   135→          locale
   136→          region
   137→          metroArea
   138→          metroDistance
   139→        }
   140→        academics {
   141→          institutionType
   142→          highestDegree
   143→          enrollment
   144→          gradEnrollment
   145→          studentFacultyRatio
   146→          fullTimeFacultyRate
   147→          gender
   148→          onlineOnly
   149→        }
   150→        cost {
   151→          avgNetPrice
   152→          tuitionInState
   153→          tuitionOutOfState
   154→          roomAndBoard
   155→          avgNetPriceLowIncome
   156→          pellGrantRate
   157→          federalLoanRate
   158→          endowment
   159→        }
   160→        outcomes {
   161→          admissionRate
   162→          graduationRate
   163→          retentionRate
   164→          transferRate
   165→        }
   166→        rankings {
   167→          usNewsNational
   168→          usNewsLiberalArts
   169→          forbes
   170→          washingtonMonthlyNational
   171→          washingtonMonthlyLiberalArts
   172→          niche
   173→          moneyScore
   174→        }
   175→        admissions {
   176→          applicationPlatforms
   177→        }
   178→        athletics {
   179→          ncaaDivision
   180→          ncaaConference
   181→        }
   182→        climate {
   183→          koppenClimate
   184→          koppenTemperature
   185→          koppenPrecipitation
   186→        }
   187→        culture {
   188→          partisanDirection
   189→          partisanScore
   190→        }
   191→      }
   192→      total
   193→      limit
   194→      offset
   195→    }
   196→  }
   197→`)
   198→
   199→// ── Search page ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   200→
   201→export function Search() {
   202→  const [searchParams, setSearchParams] = useSearchParams()
   203→  const [selectedUnitID, setSelectedUnitID] = useState<string | null>(null)
   204→  const [detailOpened, setDetailOpened] = useState(false)
   205→  const [filtersOpened, { toggle: toggleFilters }] = useDisclosure(false)
   206→  const isDesktop = useMediaQuery('(min-width: 62em)')
   207→
   208→  // ── View mode state ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   209→  const [viewMode, setViewMode] = useState<ViewMode>(() => {
   210→    const fromUrl = searchParams.get('view')
   211→    if (fromUrl && VALID_VIEW_MODES.includes(fromUrl as ViewMode)) return fromUrl as ViewMode
   212→    const fromStorage = localStorage.getItem(VIEW_MODE_KEY)
   213→    if (fromStorage && VALID_VIEW_MODES.includes(fromStorage as ViewMode))
   214→      return fromStorage as ViewMode
   215→    return 'card'
   216→  })
   217→
   218→  const handleViewModeChange = useCallback(
   219→    (mode: ViewMode) => {
   220→      setViewMode(mode)
   221→      localStorage.setItem(VIEW_MODE_KEY, mode)
   222→      setSearchParams(
   223→        (prev) => {
   224→          const next = new URLSearchParams(prev)
   225→          if (mode === 'card') {
   226→            next.delete('view')
   227→          } else {
   228→            next.set('view', mode)
   229→          }
   230→          return next
   231→        },
   232→        { replace: true },
   233→      )
   234→    },
   235→    [setSearchParams],
   236→  )
   237→
   238→  const dataSources = useDataSources()
   239→
   240→  const [fieldsResult] = useQuery<{ searchFields: SearchField[] }>({
   241→    query: SearchFieldsQuery,
   242→  })
   243→
   244→  const [outputFieldsResult] = useQuery<{ searchOutputFields: SearchOutputField[] }>({
   245→    query: SearchOutputFieldsQuery,
   246→  })
   247→
   248→  const fields = useMemo(
   249→    () => fieldsResult.data?.searchFields ?? [],
   250→    [fieldsResult.data?.searchFields],
   251→  )
   252→
   253→  const allOutputFields = useMemo(
   254→    () => outputFieldsResult.data?.searchOutputFields ?? [],
   255→    [outputFieldsResult.data?.searchOutputFields],
   256→  )
   257→
   258→  // ── Column visibility state ─────────────────────────────────────────────
   259→  const [visibleColumns, setVisibleColumns] = useState<Set<string>>(() => {
   260→    const stored = localStorage.getItem(VISIBLE_COLUMNS_KEY)
   261→    if (stored) {
   262→      try {
   263→        const arr = JSON.parse(stored) as string[]
   264→        if (Array.isArray(arr) && arr.length > 0) return new Set(arr)
   265→      } catch {
   266→        // ignore parse errors
   267→      }
   268→    }
   269→    return new Set<string>()
   270→  })
   271→
   272→  // ── Column order state (excludes pinned "name" column) ────────────────
   273→  const [columnOrder, setColumnOrder] = useState<string[]>(() => {
   274→    const stored = localStorage.getItem(COLUMN_ORDER_KEY)
   275→    if (stored) {
   276→      try {
   277→        const arr = JSON.parse(stored) as string[]
   278→        if (Array.isArray(arr) && arr.length > 0) return arr
   279→      } catch {
   280→        // ignore parse errors
   281→      }
   282→    }
   283→    return []
   284→  })
   285→
   286→  // Initialize from defaultVisible when output fields load
   287→  useEffect(() => {
   288→    if (allOutputFields.length > 0 && visibleColumns.size === 0) {
   289→      const defaults = new Set(
   290→        allOutputFields.filter((f) => f.defaultVisible).map((f) => f.name),
   291→      )
   292→      // Always include core fields
   293→      defaults.add('name')
   294→      defaults.add('city')
   295→      defaults.add('state')
   296→      setVisibleColumns(defaults)
   297→    }
   298→  }, [allOutputFields, visibleColumns.size])
   299→
   300→  // Initialize column order when output fields load and no saved order exists
   301→  useEffect(() => {
   302→    if (allOutputFields.length > 0 && columnOrder.length === 0 && visibleColumns.size > 0) {
   303→      // Default order: city, state, distance, myPrice, then other visible fields
   304→      const defaultOrder = ['city', 'state', 'distance', 'myPrice']
   305→      const outputFieldsByName = new Map(allOutputFields.map((f) => [f.name, f]))
   306→      for (const name of visibleColumns) {
   307→        if (name !== 'name' && !defaultOrder.includes(name) && outputFieldsByName.has(name)) {
   308→          defaultOrder.push(name)
   309→        }
   310→      }
   311→      setColumnOrder(defaultOrder)
   312→    }
   313→  }, [allOutputFields, columnOrder.length, visibleColumns])
   314→
   315→  // Persist to localStorage
   316→  useEffect(() => {
   317→    if (visibleColumns.size > 0) {
   318→      localStorage.setItem(VISIBLE_COLUMNS_KEY, JSON.stringify([...visibleColumns]))
   319→    }
   320→  }, [visibleColumns])
   321→
   322→  useEffect(() => {
   323→    if (columnOrder.length > 0) {
   324→      localStorage.setItem(COLUMN_ORDER_KEY, JSON.stringify(columnOrder))
   325→    }
   326→  }, [columnOrder])
   327→
   328→  const handleToggleColumn = useCallback((fieldName: string) => {
   329→    setVisibleColumns((prev) => {
   330→      const next = new Set(prev)
   331→      if (next.has(fieldName)) {
   332→        next.delete(fieldName)
   333→      } else {
   334→        next.add(fieldName)
   335→      }
   336→      return next
   337→    })
   338→    // Update column order: add to end when toggling on, remove when toggling off
   339→    setColumnOrder((prev) => {
   340→      if (prev.includes(fieldName)) {
   341→        return prev.filter((n) => n !== fieldName)
   342→      }
   343→      return [...prev, fieldName]
   344→    })
   345→  }, [])
   346→
   347→  const handleReorderColumn = useCallback((fieldName: string, direction: 'up' | 'down') => {
   348→    setColumnOrder((prev) => {
   349→      const idx = prev.indexOf(fieldName)
   350→      if (idx === -1) return prev
   351→      const swapIdx = direction === 'up' ? idx - 1 : idx + 1
   352→      if (swapIdx < 0 || swapIdx >= prev.length) return prev
   353→      const next = [...prev]
   354→      next[idx] = prev[swapIdx]
   355→      next[swapIdx] = prev[idx]
   356→      return next
   357→    })
   358→  }, [])
   359→
   360→  const handleSetColumnOrder = useCallback((order: string[]) => {
   361→    setColumnOrder(order)
   362→  }, [])
   363→
   364→  // Derive filter state from URL search params + field metadata
   365→  const filters = useMemo(
   366→    () => (fields.length === 0 ? {} : parseUrlToFilters(searchParams, fields)),
   367→    [searchParams, fields],
   368→  )
   369→
   370→  // User location from URL params
   371→  const userLocation = useMemo(() => {
   372→    const lat = searchParams.get('userLat')
   373→    const lng = searchParams.get('userLng')
   374→    if (lat == null || lng == null) return undefined
   375→    const latitude = Number(lat)
   376→    const longitude = Number(lng)
   377→    if (Number.isNaN(latitude) || Number.isNaN(longitude)) return undefined
   378→    const state = searchParams.get('userState') ?? undefined
   379→    return { latitude, longitude, state }
   380→  }, [searchParams])
   381→
   382→  // Sort state from URL params
   383→  const sort = useMemo((): SortState | undefined => {
   384→    const sortParam = searchParams.get('sort')
   385→    if (!sortParam) return undefined
   386→    const dirParam = searchParams.get('sortDir')
   387→    const direction = dirParam === 'DESC' ? ('DESC' as const) : ('ASC' as const)
   388→    return { field: sortParam, direction }
   389→  }, [searchParams])
   390→
   391→  // Ranking blend weights from URL params
   392→  const blendWeights = useMemo(
   393→    () => parseBlendParam(searchParams.get('blend')),
   394→    [searchParams],
   395→  )
   396→  const hasBlend = Object.keys(blendWeights).length > 0
   397→
   398→  // Pagination state from URL params
   399→  const pageSize = useMemo(() => {
   400→    const ps = Number(searchParams.get('pageSize'))
   401→    return PAGE_SIZE_OPTIONS.includes(ps) ? ps : DEFAULT_PAGE_SIZE
   402→  }, [searchParams])
   403→
   404→  const page = useMemo(() => {
   405→    const p = Number(searchParams.get('page'))
   406→    return p >= 1 ? p : 1
   407→  }, [searchParams])
   408→
   409→  const offset = (page - 1) * pageSize
   410→
   411→  const [searchResult] = useQuery<{
   412→    search: { results: SearchInstitution[]; total: number; limit: number; offset: number }
   413→  }>({
   414→    query: SearchQuery,
   415→    variables: {
   416→      filters: buildFilterInputs(filters),
   417→      limit: pageSize,
   418→      offset,
   419→      sort: hasBlend ? null : (sort ?? null),
   420→      userLocation: userLocation
   421→        ? {
   422→            latitude: userLocation.latitude,
   423→            longitude: userLocation.longitude,
   424→            state: userLocation.state,
   425→          }
   426→        : null,
   427→      rankingBlend: hasBlend
   428→        ? Object.entries(blendWeights).map(([field, weight]) => ({ field, weight }))
   429→        : null,
   430→    },
   431→    pause: fieldsResult.fetching,
   432→  })
   433→
   434→  const themeGroups = useMemo(
   435→    () => fieldsByTheme(fields.filter((f) => f.visibility !== 'HIDDEN')),
   436→    [fields],
   437→  )
   438→
   439→  const institutions = useMemo(
   440→    () => searchResult.data?.search.results ?? [],
   441→    [searchResult.data?.search.results],
   442→  )
   443→  const total = searchResult.data?.search.total ?? 0
   444→  const cappedTotal = Math.min(total, MAX_RESULTS)
   445→  const totalPages = Math.ceil(cappedTotal / pageSize)
   446→
   447→  // "Showing X–Y of Z" display values
   448→  const showStart = cappedTotal === 0 ? 0 : offset + 1
   449→  const showEnd = Math.min(offset + pageSize, cappedTotal)
   450→
   451→  const hasLocation = userLocation != null
   452→  const hasState = userLocation?.state != null
   453→
   454→  function handleFilterChange(fieldName: string, value: unknown | undefined) {
   455→    setSearchParams(
   456→      (prev) => {
   457→        const next = new URLSearchParams(prev)
   458→        // Convert legacy ?q= to canonical ?name= so the value is preserved
   459→        const q = next.get('q')
   460→        if (q) {
   461→          next.delete('q')
   462→          if (!next.has('name') && fieldName !== 'name') {
   463→            next.set('name', q)
   464→          }
   465→        }
   466→        if (value === undefined) {
   467→          next.delete(fieldName)
   468→        } else {
   469→          next.set(fieldName, serializeFilterValue(value))
   470→        }
   471→        // Reset to page 1 when filters change
   472→        next.delete('page')
   473→        return next
   474→      },
   475→      { replace: true },
   476→    )
   477→  }
   478→
   479→  const handleLocationChange = useCallback(
   480→    (loc: UserLocationValue | undefined) => {
   481→      setSearchParams(
   482→        (prev) => {
   483→          const next = new URLSearchParams(prev)
   484→          if (loc) {
   485→            next.set('userLat', String(loc.latitude))
   486→            next.set('userLng', String(loc.longitude))
   487→            if (loc.state) {
   488→              next.set('userState', loc.state)
   489→            } else {
   490→              next.delete('userState')
   491→            }
   492→          } else {
   493→            next.delete('userLat')
   494→            next.delete('userLng')
   495→            next.delete('userState')
   496→            if (next.get('sort') === 'distance') {
   497→              next.delete('sort')
   498→              next.delete('sortDir')
   499→            }
   500→          }
   501→          next.delete('page')
   502→          return next
   503→        },
   504→        { replace: true },
   505→      )
   506→    },
   507→    [setSearchParams],
   508→  )
   509→
   510→  const handleSort = useCallback(
   511→    (field: string) => {
   512→      setSearchParams(
   513→        (prev) => {
   514→          const next = new URLSearchParams(prev)
   515→          const currentField = next.get('sort')
   516→          const currentDir = next.get('sortDir') ?? 'ASC'
   517→
   518→          if (currentField === field) {
   519→            if (currentDir === 'ASC') {
   520→              // ASC → DESC
   521→              next.set('sortDir', 'DESC')
   522→            } else {
   523→              // DESC → clear
   524→              next.delete('sort')
   525→              next.delete('sortDir')
   526→            }
   527→          } else {
   528→            // New field → ASC
   529→            next.set('sort', field)
   530→            next.delete('sortDir')
   531→          }
   532→          next.delete('page')
   533→          return next
   534→        },
   535→        { replace: true },
   536→      )
   537→    },
   538→    [setSearchParams],
   539→  )
   540→
   541→  const handleBlendChange = useCallback(
   542→    (weights: Record<string, number>) => {
   543→      setSearchParams(
   544→        (prev) => {
   545→          const next = new URLSearchParams(prev)
   546→          const serialized = serializeBlendWeights(weights)
   547→          if (serialized) {
   548→            next.set('blend', serialized)
   549→            // Clear sort when blend is active — blend determines ordering
   550→            next.delete('sort')
   551→            next.delete('sortDir')
   552→          } else {
   553→            next.delete('blend')
   554→          }
   555→          next.delete('page')
   556→          return next
   557→        },
   558→        { replace: true },
   559→      )
   560→    },
   561→    [setSearchParams],
   562→  )
   563→
   564→  function handlePageChange(newPage: number) {
   565→    setSearchParams(
   566→      (prev) => {
   567→        const next = new URLSearchParams(prev)
   568→        if (newPage <= 1) {
   569→          next.delete('page')
   570→        } else {
   571→          next.set('page', String(newPage))
   572→        }
   573→        return next
   574→      },
   575→      { replace: true },
   576→    )
   577→  }
   578→
   579→  function handlePageSizeChange(newSize: number) {
   580→    setSearchParams(
   581→      (prev) => {
   582→        const next = new URLSearchParams(prev)
   583→        if (newSize === DEFAULT_PAGE_SIZE) {
   584→          next.delete('pageSize')
   585→        } else {
   586→          next.set('pageSize', String(newSize))
   587→        }
   588→        next.delete('page')
   589→        return next
   590→      },
   591→      { replace: true },
   592→    )
   593→  }
   594→
   595→  const handleExportCsv = useCallback(() => {
   596→    const params = new URLSearchParams()
   597→    const filterInputs = buildFilterInputs(filters)
   598→    if (filterInputs.length > 0) {
   599→      params.set('filters', JSON.stringify(filterInputs))
   600→    }
   601→    if (sort) {
   602→      params.set('sort', sort.field)
   603→    }
   604→    if (userLocation) {
   605→      params.set('userLat', String(userLocation.latitude))
   606→      params.set('userLng', String(userLocation.longitude))
   607→      if (userLocation.state) {
   608→        params.set('userState', userLocation.state)
   609→      }
   610→    }
   611→    window.open(`/api/export/csv?${params.toString()}`, '_blank')
   612→  }, [filters, sort, userLocation])
   613→
   614→  const handleExportPdf = useCallback(() => {
   615→    const activeFilters = fields
   616→      .filter((f) => filters[f.name] !== undefined)
   617→      .map((f) => f.label)
   618→    downloadSearchPdf({
   619→      institutions,
   620→      outputFields: allOutputFields,
   621→      activeFilters,
   622→      maxRows: MAX_RESULTS,
   623→      dataSources,
   624→    })
   625→  }, [institutions, allOutputFields, fields, filters, dataSources])
   626→
   627→  const filterContent = (
   628→    <>
   629→      <Title order={6} tt="uppercase" fz="xs" fw={600} lts="0.05em" c="brand.9" mb="md">
   630→        Filters
   631→      </Title>
   632→
   633→      <Box mb="md">
   634→        <Text size="xs" fw={600} tt="uppercase" lts="0.05em" c="dimmed" mb="xs">
   635→          Your Location
   636→        </Text>
   637→        <LocationInput
   638→          value={
   639→            userLocation
   640→              ? {
   641→                  latitude: userLocation.latitude,
   642→                  longitude: userLocation.longitude,
   643→                  state: userLocation.state,
   644→                }
   645→              : undefined
   646→          }
   647→          onChange={handleLocationChange}
   648→        />
   649→      </Box>
   650→
   651→      <Box mb="md">
   652→        <RankingBlendBuilder
   653→          weights={blendWeights}
   654→          onChange={handleBlendChange}
   655→          fields={fields}
   656→        />
   657→      </Box>
   658→
   659→      {fieldsResult.fetching && (
   660→        <Text size="sm" c="dimmed">
   661→          Loading filters…
   662→        </Text>
   663→      )}
   664→      {fieldsResult.error && (
   665→        <Text size="sm" c="red">
   666→          Failed to load filters.
   667→        </Text>
   668→      )}
   669→      {themeGroups.map((group) => (
   670→        <Box key={group.theme} mb="md">
   671→          <Text size="xs" fw={600} tt="uppercase" lts="0.05em" c="dimmed" mb="xs">
   672→            {group.label}
   673→          </Text>
   674→          {group.fields.map((field) => (
   675→            <FilterControl
   676→              key={field.name}
   677→              field={field}
   678→              value={filters[field.name]}
   679→              onChange={(v) => handleFilterChange(field.name, v)}
   680→              dataSources={dataSources}
   681→            />
   682→          ))}
   683→        </Box>
   684→      ))}
   685→    </>
   686→  )
   687→
   688→  return (
   689→    <Box component="main" style={{ display: 'flex', flex: 1, flexDirection: 'column' }}>
   690→      <Container size="xl" px="md" py="md" style={{ display: 'flex', flex: 1 }}>
   691→        <Flex
   692→          direction={{ base: 'column', md: 'row' }}
   693→          gap={0}
   694→          style={{ flex: 1, width: '100%' }}
   695→        >
   696→          {/* Sidebar: on mobile, collapsible; on desktop, fixed-width aside */}
   697→          <Box
   698→            style={{
   699→              ...(isDesktop
   700→                ? {
   701→                    width: 280,
   702→                    flexShrink: 0,
   703→                    overflowY: 'auto',
   704→                    borderRight: '1px solid var(--mantine-color-gray-3)',
   705→                    paddingRight: 16,
   706→                  }
   707→                : {}),
   708→            }}
   709→          >
   710→            {!isDesktop && (
   711→              <Box mb="sm">
   712→                <Button
   713→                  variant="default"
   714→                  size="xs"
   715→                  onClick={toggleFilters}
   716→                  leftSection={<SlidersHorizontal size={16} />}
   717→                >
   718→                  {filtersOpened ? 'Hide Filters' : 'Show Filters'}
   719→                </Button>
   720→              </Box>
   721→            )}
   722→
   723→            {isDesktop ? (
   724→              filterContent
   725→            ) : (
   726→              <Collapse in={filtersOpened}>
   727→                <Box
   728→                  mb="md"
   729→                  pb="md"
   730→                  style={{ borderBottom: '1px solid var(--mantine-color-gray-3)' }}
   731→                >
   732→                  {filterContent}
   733→                </Box>
   734→              </Collapse>
   735→            )}
   736→          </Box>
   737→
   738→          {/* Results section */}
   739→          <Box style={{ flex: 1, minWidth: 0 }} pl={{ base: 0, md: 'lg' }}>
   740→            <Group justify="space-between" mb="md" wrap="wrap" gap="sm">
   741→              <Title order={2} c="brand.9">
   742→                Results
   743→              </Title>
   744→              <Group gap="xs" wrap="wrap">
   745→                <ViewToggle value={viewMode} onChange={handleViewModeChange} />
   746→                {hasLocation && (
   747→                  <Button variant="default" size="xs" onClick={() => handleSort('distance')}>
   748→                    {sort?.field === 'distance' ? 'Sorting by distance' : 'Sort by distance'}
   749→                  </Button>
   750→                )}
   751→                {viewMode === 'table' && (
   752→                  <ColumnPicker
   753→                    outputFields={allOutputFields}
   754→                    visibleColumns={visibleColumns}
   755→                    onToggleColumn={handleToggleColumn}

   757→                    onReorderColumn={handleReorderColumn}
   758→                    onSetColumnOrder={handleSetColumnOrder}
   759→                  />
   760→                )}
   761→                <Button
   762→                  variant="default"
   763→                  size="xs"
   764→                  onClick={handleExportPdf}
   765→                  disabled={institutions.length === 0}
   766→                  leftSection={<FileDown size={14} />}
   767→                >
   768→                  Export PDF
   769→                </Button>
   770→                <Button
   771→                  variant="default"
   772→                  size="xs"
   773→                  onClick={handleExportCsv}
   774→                  disabled={institutions.length === 0}
   775→                  leftSection={<Download size={14} />}
   776→                >
   777→                  Export CSV
   778→                </Button>
   779→              </Group>
   780→            </Group>
   781→
   782→            {searchResult.fetching && (
   783→              <div data-testid="results-loading">
   784→                {Array.from({ length: 3 }, (_, i) => (
   785→                  <InstitutionCardSkeleton key={i} />
   786→                ))}
   787→              </div>
   788→            )}
   789→            {searchResult.error && (
   790→              <Stack align="center" py="xl" gap="sm">
   791→                <Text fz="sm" c="red">
   792→                  Something went wrong loading results.
   793→                </Text>
   794→                <Button variant="default" size="xs" onClick={() => window.location.reload()}>
   795→                  Retry
   796→                </Button>
   797→              </Stack>
   798→            )}
   799→            {!searchResult.fetching && searchResult.data && (
   800→              <Stack gap="md">
   801→                <Text fz="sm" c="dimmed" aria-live="polite">
   802→                  {total > MAX_RESULTS
   803→                    ? `Showing ${showStart}–${showEnd} of ${MAX_RESULTS}+ results`
   804→                    : `Showing ${showStart}–${showEnd} of ${total.toLocaleString()} institutions found`}
   805→                </Text>
   806→                {institutions.length === 0 ? (
   807→                  <Stack align="center" py="xl" gap="xs">
   808→                    <Text fw={500}>No colleges match your filters</Text>
   809→                    <Button
   810→                      variant="default"
   811→                      size="xs"
   812→                      style={{ color: 'var(--color-primary)' }}
   813→                      onClick={() => setSearchParams({}, { replace: true })}
   814→                    >
   815→                      Clear all filters
   816→                    </Button>
   817→                  </Stack>
   818→                ) : viewMode === 'card' ? (
   819→                  <SimpleGrid cols={{ base: 1, sm: 2, lg: 3 }} spacing="md">
   820→                    {institutions.map((inst) => (
   821→                      <InstitutionCard
   822→                        key={inst.unitID}
   823→                        institution={inst}
   824→                        hasLocation={hasLocation}
   825→                        hasState={hasState}
   826→                        onClick={() => {
   827→                          setSelectedUnitID(inst.unitID)
   828→                          setDetailOpened(true)
   829→                        }}
   830→                      />
   831→                    ))}
   832→                  </SimpleGrid>
   833→                ) : viewMode === 'list' ? (
   834→                  <ResultsList
   835→                    institutions={institutions}
   836→                    hasLocation={hasLocation}
   837→                    hasState={hasState}
   838→                    onClickInstitution={(unitID) => {
   839→                      setSelectedUnitID(unitID)
   840→                      setDetailOpened(true)
   841→                    }}
   842→                  />
   843→                ) : viewMode === 'map' ? (
   844→                  <ResultsMap
   845→                    institutions={institutions}
   846→                    userLocation={userLocation ?? undefined}
   847→                    onClickInstitution={(unitID) => {
   848→                      setSelectedUnitID(unitID)
   849→                      setDetailOpened(true)
   850→                    }}
   851→                  />
   852→                ) : (
   853→                  <Box style={{ overflowX: 'auto' }}>
   854→                    <ResultsTable
   855→                      institutions={institutions}
   856→                      outputFields={allOutputFields}
   857→                      visibleColumns={visibleColumns}
   858→                      columnOrder={columnOrder}
   859→                      onClickInstitution={(unitID) => {
   860→                        setSelectedUnitID(unitID)
   861→                        setDetailOpened(true)
   862→                      }}
   863→                      hasLocation={hasLocation}
   864→                      hasState={hasState}
   865→                      hasBlend={hasBlend}
   866→                      sort={sort}
   867→                      onSort={handleSort}
   868→                      dataSources={dataSources}
   869→                    />
   870→                  </Box>
   871→                )}
   872→
   873→                {totalPages > 1 && (
   874→                  <Group gap="md" wrap="wrap" data-testid="pagination-controls">
   875→                    <Pagination total={totalPages} value={page} onChange={handlePageChange} />
   876→                    <Group gap="xs">
   877→                      <NativeSelect
   878→                        id="page-size"
   879→                        aria-label="Per page"
   880→                        value={String(pageSize)}
   881→                        onChange={(e) => handlePageSizeChange(Number(e.target.value))}
   882→                        data={PAGE_SIZE_OPTIONS.map((size) => ({
   883→                          value: String(size),
   884→                          label: String(size),
   885→                        }))}
   886→                        size="xs"
   887→                        style={{ width: 70 }}
   888→                      />
   889→                      <Text component="label" htmlFor="page-size" fz="sm" c="dimmed">
   890→                        Per page
   891→                      </Text>
   892→                    </Group>
   893→                  </Group>
   894→                )}
   895→              </Stack>
   896→            )}
   897→          </Box>
   898→        </Flex>
   899→      </Container>
   900→
   901→      <InstitutionDetail
   902→        unitID={selectedUnitID}
   903→        opened={detailOpened}
   904→        onClose={() => setDetailOpened(false)}
   905→      />
   906→    </Box>
   907→  )
   908→}

    74→  return render(

    78→  )
    79→}

1→import type { DemoStep } from './types'
     2→
     3→const BASE_URL = 'http://localhost:8080'
     4→
     5→export const steps: DemoStep[] = [
     6→  {
     7→    title: 'Welcome',
     8→    description: 'This is College Power Search — a faceted search engine for U.S. colleges.',
     9→    action: async (page) => {
    10→      await page.goto(BASE_URL, { waitUntil: 'networkidle' })
    11→      // Scroll to make sure the hero is fully visible
    12→      await page.evaluate(() => window.scrollTo({ top: 0, behavior: 'smooth' }))
    13→    },
    14→  },
    15→
    16→  {
    17→    title: 'Quick Search',
    18→    description: 'Type a college name into the search box to start narrowing results.',
    19→    action: async (page) => {
    20→      const input = page.locator('input[type="search"]')
    21→      await input.scrollIntoViewIfNeeded()
    22→      await input.click()
    23→      await input.type('Stanford', { delay: 80 })
    24→    },
    25→  },
    26→
    27→  {
    28→    title: 'Add Filters',
    29→    description: 'Use the dropdowns to filter by state and ranking tier.',
    30→    action: async (page) => {
    31→      // Select California from the state dropdown
    32→      const stateSelect = page.locator('select').filter({ hasText: 'Any state' })
    33→      await stateSelect.selectOption('CA')
    34→      await page.waitForTimeout(400)
    35→
    36→      // Select Top 50 ranking
    37→      const rankSelect = page.locator('select').filter({ hasText: 'Any rank' })
    38→      await rankSelect.selectOption('50')
    39→    },
    40→  },
    41→
    42→  {
    43→    title: 'Submit Search',
    44→    description: 'Click "Search" to see matching institutions on the results page.',
    45→    action: async (page) => {
    46→      await page.locator('button[type="submit"]:has-text("Search")').click()
    47→      await page.waitForURL('**/search?**', { timeout: 10000 })
    48→      // Wait for results table to load
    49→      await page.waitForSelector('table', { timeout: 10000 }).catch(() => {
    50→        // Results may be empty — that's OK for the demo
    51→      })
    52→    },
    53→  },
    54→
    55→  {
    56→    title: 'Browse Results',
    57→    description: 'Results appear in a data table with name, city, state, and more columns.',
    58→    action: async (page) => {
    59→      const table = page.locator('table')
    60→      await table.scrollIntoViewIfNeeded()
    61→      // Highlight rows one by one
    62→      await page.evaluate(async () => {
    63→        const rows = document.querySelectorAll('table tbody tr')
    64→        for (const row of rows) {
    65→          ;(row as HTMLElement).style.transition = 'background-color 0.3s'
    66→          ;(row as HTMLElement).style.backgroundColor = 'rgba(201, 162, 39, 0.15)'
    67→          await new Promise((r) => setTimeout(r, 400))
    68→          ;(row as HTMLElement).style.backgroundColor = ''
    69→        }
    70→      })
    71→    },
    72→  },
    73→
    74→  {
    75→    title: 'Ranking Blend',
    76→    description: 'Create a custom ranking by weighting multiple ranking providers together.',
    77→    action: async (page) => {
    78→      await page.goto(`${BASE_URL}/search`, { waitUntil: 'networkidle' })
    79→      await page.waitForTimeout(500)
    80→
    81→      const blendSection = page.locator('[data-testid="ranking-blend-builder"]')
    82→      if (await blendSection.isVisible().catch(() => false)) {
    83→        await blendSection.scrollIntoViewIfNeeded()
    84→        await page.waitForTimeout(300)
    85→
    86→        // Highlight the blend builder area
    87→        await page.evaluate(() => {
    88→          const section = document.querySelector('[data-testid="ranking-blend-builder"]')
    89→          if (!section) return
    90→          ;(section as HTMLElement).style.transition = 'box-shadow 0.3s'
    91→          ;(section as HTMLElement).style.boxShadow = '0 0 0 3px rgba(201, 162, 39, 0.5)'
    92→          setTimeout(() => {
    93→            ;(section as HTMLElement).style.boxShadow = ''
    94→          }, 2500)
    95→        })
    96→        await page.waitForTimeout(2500)
    97→      }
    98→    },
    99→  },
   100→
   101→  {
   102→    title: 'Column Picker',
   103→    description: 'Use the Columns button to show or hide data columns in the results table.',
   104→    action: async (page) => {

content.text:
Excellent! Now I have all the information needed. Let me provide you with a comprehensive summary:

## Search Results Rendering Summary

### 1. **HTML Elements Used for Results**

The search results use **multiple view modes**, each with different HTML structures:

- **Card View** (default): Uses Mantine `<Card>` components in a `SimpleGrid` layout
- **List View**: Uses Mantine `<Stack>` containing `<Box>` elements styled as rows (NOT a semantic list element)
- **Table View**: Uses Mantine's `<Table>` component (native HTML `<table>`)
- **Map View**: Uses Leaflet `<MapContainer>` with markers

**There is NO dedicated `<ul>` or `<ol>` element** — each view uses its own semantic structure.

### 2. **Components That Render Results**

Located in `/home/ketan/project/kapow/web/src/components/search/`:

| Component | File | Renders |
|-----------|------|---------|
| **Search Page** | `Search.tsx` | Main orchestrator; conditionally renders one of: |
| **InstitutionCard** | `InstitutionCard.tsx` | Individual card (Mantine `Card`) |
| **ResultsList** | `ResultsList.tsx` | List view (Stack of Box elements with buttons) |
| **ResultsTable** | `ResultsTable.tsx` | Table view (Mantine `Table`) |
| **ResultsMap** | `ResultsMap.tsx` | Map view (Leaflet `MapContainer`) |

**Key passage from `Search.tsx` (lines 818-871):**
```tsx
{institutions.length === 0 ? (
  // No results UI
) : viewMode === 'card' ? (
  <SimpleGrid cols={{ base: 1, sm: 2, lg: 3 }} spacing="md">
    {institutions.map((inst) => <InstitutionCard ... />)}
  </SimpleGrid>
) : viewMode === 'list' ? (
  <ResultsList ... />
) : viewMode === 'map' ? (
  <ResultsMap ... />
) : (
  <Box style={{ overflowX: 'auto' }}>
    <ResultsTable ... />
  </Box>
)}
```

### 3. **Reliable Selectors for Results Container**

The results container itself is wrapped in a `<Box>` element with `flex: 1` at line 739 of `Search.tsx`. However, **there's no explicit data-testid on the main results container**. Here are the reliable selectors:

#### **For Card View:**
```css
/* The SimpleGrid containing cards */
[class*="SimpleGrid"] > [class*="Card"]

/* Individual cards */
[class*="Card"][role="button"]
```

#### **For List View:**
```css
/* The Stack containing list rows */
[class*="Stack"] > [role="button"]

/* Individual rows */
[role="button"][tabindex="0"]
```

#### **For Table View (most reliable):**
```css
/* The table element */
table

/* Table body rows */
table tbody tr

/* Individual cells */
table tbody tr > td
```

#### **For Map View:**
```css
/* Leaflet container */
.leaflet-container

/* Markers */
.leaflet-marker-pane > img
```

#### **Universal for all views:**
```css
/* Results section heading (present in all modes) */
h2:has-text("Results")

/* Pagination controls (present when > 1 page) */
[data-testid="pagination-controls"]

/* Loading skeleton (card view) */
[data-testid="result-skeleton"]
[data-testid="results-loading"]
```

### 4. **Key Data-TestID Attributes**

From the test files, these are the reliable attributes:

| Element | Selector |
|---------|----------|
| Pagination controls | `[data-testid="pagination-controls"]` |
| Distance in list/table | `[data-testid="distance"]` |
| My Price in list/table | `[data-testid="my-price"]` |
| Blend Score in table | `[data-testid="blend-score"]` |
| Sort button for field | `[data-testid="sort-{fieldName}"]` |
| Theme group header | `[data-testid="theme-group-{THEME}"]` |
| Results loading | `[data-testid="results-loading"]` |
| Card skeleton | `[data-testid="result-skeleton"]` |
| Ranking blend builder | `[data-testid="ranking-blend-builder"]` |

### 5. **File Paths**

- Search page: `/home/ketan/project/kapow/web/src/pages/Search.tsx`
- Results list: `/home/ketan/project/kapow/web/src/components/search/ResultsList.tsx`
- Results table: `/home/ketan/project/kapow/web/src/components/search/ResultsTable.tsx`
- Institution card: `/home/ketan/project/kapow/web/src/components/search/InstitutionCard.tsx`
- Results map: `/home/ketan/project/kapow/web/src/components/search/ResultsMap.tsx`

### 6. **Demo Integration**

The `demo/steps.ts` file references results elements:
- Line 49: Waits for `<table>` element specifically
- Line 59: Uses `page.locator('table')` to locate results in demo
- Lines 62-70: Iterates over `document.querySelectorAll('table tbody tr')` to highlight rows

This confirms the **table view is the primary focus for automated interactions**.

content.text:
Now I understand the full picture. Let me address your question before making changes.

---

That's a great architectural question. The demo and e2e tests serve different purposes but share the same interaction patterns — navigating pages, filling filters, checking results. Two approaches:

**Option A: Shared step library, two consumers.** Extract reusable page-object helpers (e.g. `searchFor("Stanford", "CA")`, `switchToTableView()`, `waitForResults()`) into a shared module. Both the demo steps and e2e tests import from it. The demo adds overlay/pause logic on top; e2e adds assertions.

**Option B: Demo as the primary, e2e derives from it.** Each demo step becomes a test — run the step action, then assert expected state. The demo is the "golden path" walkthrough; e2e tests replay it headlessly with assertions bolted on.

Option A is cleaner — the demo is presentation-focused (pauses, highlights, overlays) while tests are assertion-focused. Forcing them into one sequence makes both worse. But sharing the interaction primitives (page objects) eliminates duplication.

For now though, the immediate bug is that `steps.ts` assumes table view but the default is card view. Want me to:

1. **Just fix the demo** — switch to table view before the "Browse Results" step (quick fix)
2. **Refactor to shared page objects** — extract helpers used by both demo and e2e, fix selectors in the process (bigger but more sustainable)

Which do you prefer?

message.content: the goal is to know that, if the end-to-end tests pass, the demo will pass.  sharing code kind of helps, but I'm looking for a stronger guarantee.  fixing the demo seems like it's the right thing to do regardless.

content.thinking:
The user wants:
1. Fix the demo now (immediate bug fix)
2. A stronger guarantee than just shared code - they want e2e tests that actually exercise the same paths as the demo, so passing e2e tests implies the demo will work

Let me think about the strongest guarantee: the demo steps themselves ARE the e2e tests. Each demo step's action runs, then an assertion validates the expected state. The demo runner adds overlays/pauses on top. The e2e test runner just runs the actions + assertions headlessly.

So the architecture would be:
- `demo/steps.ts` defines steps with `action` (what to do) and optionally `verify` (what to assert)


- The demo runner executes each action, displays an overlay, and waits for user advancement, while the e2e test runner executes the same actions and verifications headlessly with assertions

I should fix the immediate bug first, then look at what e2e tests already exist to understand how to integrate this approach.

content.text:
You're right — the strongest guarantee is that e2e tests literally run the demo steps. Each step has an `action`; e2e tests execute those same actions (plus assertions). If e2e passes, the demo actions are proven to work.

Let me fix the demo first, then look at how to wire the e2e tests.

import type { Page } from '@playwright/test'

export type DemoMode = 'auto' | 'manual'

export interface DemoStep {
  title: string
  description: string
  action: (page: Page) => Promise<void>
}

export interface DemoOptions {
  mode: DemoMode
  pauseMs: number
  startStep: number
  browser: 'chromium' | 'firefox' | 'webkit'
  headless: boolean
}

1→import type { Page } from '@playwright/test' 2→import type { DemoMode } from './types' 3→ 4→/** 5→ * Injects or updates the demo overlay panel on the page. 6→ * Must be called before each step since navigations destroy injected DOM. 7→ */ 8→export async function showOverlay( 9→ page: Page, 10→ opts: { 11→ stepIndex: number 12→ totalSteps: number 13→ title: string 14→ description: string 15→ mode: DemoMode 16→ pauseMs: number 17→ }, 18→) { 19→ await page.evaluate( 20→ ({ stepIndex, totalSteps, title, description, mode, pauseMs }) => { 21→ // Remove existing overlay if present 22→ document.getElementById('demo-overlay')?.remove() 23→ 24→ const panel = document.createElement('div') 25→ panel.id = 'demo-overlay' 26→ Object.assign(panel.style, { 27→ position: 'fixed', 28→ bottom: '24px', 29→ right: '24px', 30→ width: '340px', 31→ backgroundColor: 'rgba(14, 31, 64, 0.95)', 32→ color: '#fff', 33→ borderRadius: '12px', 34→ padding: '20px', 35→ fontFamily: 'system-ui, -apple-system, sans-serif', 36→ zIndex: '99999', 37→ boxShadow: '0 8px 32px rgba(0,0,0,0.3)', 38→ backdropFilter: 'blur(8px)', 39→ }) 40→ 41→ const progress = ((stepIndex + 1) / totalSteps) * 100 42→ 43→ panel.innerHTML = ` 44→
45→ 46→ Step ${stepIndex + 1} of ${totalSteps} 47→ 48→ ${Math.round(progress)}% 49→
50→

${title}
54→
${description}

` 74→ } 75→ ` 76→ 77→ document.body.appendChild(panel) 78→ 79→ // Set up advance mechanism 80→ if (mode === 'manual') { 81→ ;(window as Record).__demoAdvance = false 82→ document.getElementById('demo-advance-btn')?.addEventListener('click', () => { 83→ ;(window as Record).__demoAdvance = true 84→ }) 85→ } 86→ }, 87→ opts, 88→ ) 89→} 90→ 91→/** 92→ * Wait for the observer to advance — either by timeout (auto) or button click (manual). 93→ */ 94→export async function waitForAdvance(page: Page, mode: DemoMode, pauseMs: number) { 95→ if (mode === 'manual') { 96→ await page.waitForFunction(() => (window as Record).__demoAdvance === true, { 97→ timeout: 0, 98→ }) 99→ } else { 100→ // Start the countdown animation 101→ await page.evaluate(() => { 102→ const fill = document.getElementById('demo-countdown-fill') 103→ if (fill) fill.style.width = '0%' 104→ }) 105→ await page.waitForTimeout(pauseMs) 106→ } 107→} 108→

1→/**
     2→ * Playwright global setup — checks that the dev server is reachable before
     3→ * running any e2e tests. If localhost:8080 is not responding, all tests are
     4→ * skipped with a clear message instead of timing out or failing noisily.
     5→ *
     6→ * Start the dev server manually (`pnpm dev` or `make dev`) before running
     7→ * e2e tests.
     8→ */
     9→async function globalSetup() {
    10→  const baseURL = 'http://localhost:8080'
    11→
    12→  try {
    13→    const res = await fetch(baseURL, { signal: AbortSignal.timeout(3_000) })
    14→    if (!res.ok) {
    15→      throw new Error(`Dev server responded with status ${res.status}`)
    16→    }
    17→  } catch {
    18→    console.log(
    19→      `\n⏭  Skipping e2e tests — dev server not available at ${baseURL}.\n` +
    20→        `   Start it with: pnpm dev  (or make dev)\n`,
    21→    )
    22→    process.exit(0)
    23→  }
    24→}
    25→
    26→export default globalSetup
    27→

1→import { Box, Table, UnstyledButton, Group, Text } from '@mantine/core'

    14→  institutions: SearchInstitution[]

    26→
    27→function formatDistance(distance: number | null | undefined): string {
    28→  if (distance === null || distance === undefined) return '—'
    29→  if (distance < 1) return '< 1 mi'
    30→  return `${Math.round(distance).toLocaleString()} mi`
    31→}
    32→
    33→function formatMyPrice(price: number | null | undefined): string {
    34→  if (price === null || price === undefined) return '—'
    35→  return `$${Math.round(price).toLocaleString()}`
    36→}
    37→
    38→function SortIcon({ field, sort }: { field: string; sort?: SortState }) {
    39→  if (sort?.field !== field) return <ArrowUpDown size={14} opacity={0.3} />
    40→  if (sort.direction === 'ASC') return <ArrowUp size={14} />
    41→  return <ArrowDown size={14} />
    42→}
    43→
    44→function SortableHeader({
    45→  label,
    46→  field,
    47→  sort,
    48→  onSort,
    49→  style,
    50→  source,
    51→}: {
    52→  label: string
    53→  field: string
    54→  sort?: SortState
    55→  onSort?: (field: string) => void
    56→  style?: React.CSSProperties
    57→  source?: DataSource
    58→}) {
    59→  if (!onSort) {
    60→    return (
    61→      <Table.Th style={style}>
    62→        <Group gap={4} wrap="nowrap">
    63→          {label}
    64→          <DataSourceTooltip source={source} />
    65→        </Group>
    66→      </Table.Th>
    67→    )
    68→  }
    69→  return (
    70→    <Table.Th style={{ ...style, cursor: 'pointer', userSelect: 'none' }}>
    71→      <UnstyledButton onClick={() => onSort(field)} data-testid={`sort-${field}`}>
    72→        <Group gap={4} wrap="nowrap">
    73→          <Text size="sm" fw={600} component="span">
    74→            {label}
    75→          </Text>
    76→          <SortIcon field={field} sort={sort} />
    77→          <DataSourceTooltip source={source} />
    78→        </Group>
    79→      </UnstyledButton>
    80→    </Table.Th>
    81→  )
    82→}
    83→
    84→function formatBlendScore(score: number | null | undefined): string {
    85→  if (score === null || score === undefined) return '—'
    86→  return (score * 100).toFixed(1)
    87→}
    88→
    89→export function ResultsTable({
    90→  institutions,
    91→  outputFields,
    92→  visibleColumns,
    93→  columnOrder,
    94→  onClickInstitution,
    95→  hasLocation,
    96→  hasState,
    97→  hasBlend,
    98→  sort,
    99→  onSort,
   100→  dataSources,
   101→}: ResultsTableProps) {
   102→  // Build a lookup of output fields by name
   103→  const fieldsByName = new Map(outputFields.map((f) => [f.name, f]))
   104→
   105→  // Special columns have custom rendering and conditional visibility
   106→  const specialRenderers = new Set(['city', 'state', 'distance', 'myPrice'])
   107→
   108→  // Determine visibility of special conditional columns
   109→  const showDistance = hasLocation
   110→  const showMyPrice = hasState
   111→
   112→  // Check if a column should be visible
   113→  function isColumnVisible(name: string): boolean {
   114→    if (name === 'name') return false // pinned, handled separately
   115→    if (name === 'distance') return showDistance
   116→    if (name === 'myPrice') return showMyPrice
   117→    if (specialRenderers.has(name)) return visibleColumns.has(name)
   118→    return visibleColumns.has(name) && fieldsByName.has(name)
   119→  }
   120→
   121→  // Use columnOrder to determine column sequence (excluding pinned "name")
   122→  const orderedColumns = columnOrder.filter(isColumnVisible)
   123→
   124→  // Extra fields are ordered columns that aren't special-rendered
   125→  const orderedExtraFields = orderedColumns
   126→    .filter((name) => !specialRenderers.has(name))
   127→    .map((name) => fieldsByName.get(name)!)
   128→
   129→  // Group consecutive extra fields by theme for the spanning header row
   130→  const themeGroups: { theme: string; label: string; fields: SearchOutputField[] }[] = []
   131→  for (const field of orderedExtraFields) {
   132→    const last = themeGroups[themeGroups.length - 1]
   133→    if (last && last.theme === field.theme) {
   134→      last.fields.push(field)
